note: Course-level totals from race websites / runner manual. Per-mile vert differs; Tahoe front-loads climbing differently than Bighorn.

through_50_miles.note: Approximate — use CalTopo for segment-level prep. Tahoe to mile 52 is not the same profile as Bighorn to Jaws.
through_50_miles.tahoe_to_sierra_ft_gain_order: Check CalTopo; first 52 mi includes Heavenly climbs but spread over longer distance

summary: Tahoe 200 has ~11,600 ft MORE total climbing than Bighorn 100 (~32k vs ~20.5k), but spread over 2× the miles (~160 ft/mi vs ~205 ft/mi). Bighorn is steeper per mile and more remote/technical. Reaching Tahoe mile 52 by midnight is slightly more time than your Bighorn 50 mi split (15 h vs 14:20) — reasonable buffer, not a vert-equivalent guarantee.
